name: go
description: Go is a tool for managing Go source code

options.name: -n
options.description: Print the commands but do not run them

options.name: -v
options.description: Print the names of packages as they are compiled

options.name: -x
options.description: Print the commands

options.name: -tags
options.description: A comma-separated list of build tags to consider satisfied during the build

options.name: -toolexec
options.description: A program to use to invoke toolchain programs like vet and asm

options.name: -a
options.description: Force rebuilding of packages that are already up-to-date

options.name: -p
options.description: The number of programs, such as build commands or

options.name: -race
options.description:
Enable data race detection.
Supported only on linux/amd64, freebsd/amd64, darwin/amd64, windows/amd64,
linux/ppc64le and linux/arm64 (only for 48-bit VMA)

options.name: -msan
options.description:
Enable interoperation with memory sanitizer.
Supported only on linux/amd64, linux/arm64
and only with Clang/LLVM as the host C compiler.
On linux/arm64, pie build mode will be used

options.name: -work
options.description:
Print the name of the temporary work directory and
do not delete it when exiting

options.name: -asmflags
options.description: Arguments to pass on each go tool asm invocation

options.name: -buildmode
options.description: Build mode to use. See 'go help buildmode' for more

options.name: -compiler
options.description: Name of compiler to use, as in runtime.Compiler (gccgo or gc)

options.name: -gccgoflags
options.description: Arguments to pass on each gccgo compiler/linker invocation

options.name: -gcflags
options.description: Arguments to pass on each go tool compile invocation

options.name: -installsuffix
options.description: A suffix to use in the name of the package installation directory,

options.name: -ldflags
options.description: Arguments to pass on each go tool link invocation

options.name: -linkshared
options.description: Build code that will be linked against shared libraries previously

options.name: -mod
options.description: Module download mode to use: readonly, vendor, or mod

options.name: -modcacherw
options.description: Leave newly-created directories in the module cache read-write

options.name: -modfile
options.description: In module aware mode, read (and possibly write) an alternate go.mod file instead of the one in the module root directory

options.name: -overlay
options.description: Read a JSON config file that provides an overlay for build operations

options.name: -pkgdir
options.description: Install and load all packages from dir instead of the usual locations

options.name: -trimpath
options.description: Remove all file system paths from the resulting executable

options.name: -o
options.description: Write the resulting executable or object to the named output file or directory

options.name: -i
options.description: Install the packages that are dependencies of the target

subcommands.name: clean
subcommands.description: Remove object files and cached files

options.name: -i
options.description: Remove the corresponding installed archive or binary

options.name: -r
options.description: Apply recursively to all the dependencies of the packages named by the import paths

options.name: -cache
options.description: Remove the entire go build cache

options.name: -testcache
options.description: Expire all test results in the go build cache

options.name: -modcache
options.description: Remove the entire module download cache, including unpacked source code of versioned dependencies

subcommands.name: doc
subcommands.description: Show documentation for package or symbol

options.name: -all
options.description: Show all the documentation for the package

options.name: -c
options.description: Respect case when matching symbols

options.name: -cmd
options.description: Treat a command (package main) like a regular package. Otherwise package main's exported symbols are hidden when showing the package's top-level documentation

options.name: -short
options.description: One-line representation for each symbol

options.name: -src
options.description: Show the full source code for the symbol

options.name: -u
options.description: Show documentation for unexported as well as exported symbols, methods, and fields

options.name: -json
options.description: Prints the environment in JSON format instead of as a shell script

options.name: -u
options.description: Unset the default setting for the named environment variables

options.name: -w
options.description: Change the default settings of the named environment variables to the given values

subcommands.name: fix
subcommands.description: Update packages to use new APIs

options.name: -n
options.description: Print the commands that would be executed

options.name: -x
options.description: Print the commands as they are executed

options.name: -mod
options.description: Which module download mode to use

subcommands.name: generate
subcommands.description: Generate Go files by processing source

options.name: -compiled
options.description: Set CompiledGoFiles to the Go source files

options.name: -deps
options.description: Terate over not just the named packages but also all their dependencies

options.name: -f
options.description: Specify an alternate format for the list

options.name: -e
options.description: Processes the erroneous packages with the usual printing

options.name: -export
options.description: Set the Export field to the name of a file containing up-to-date export information for the given package

options.name: -find
options.description: Identify the named packages but not resolve their dependencies

options.name: -test
options.description: Report test binaries

options.name: -m
options.description: List modules instead of packages

options.name: -u
options.description: Add information about available upgrades

options.name: -versions
options.description: Set the Module's Versions field to a list of all known versions of that module

options.name: -retracted
options.description: Eport information about retracted module versions

subcommands.name: download
subcommands.description: Download the named modules into the module cache

options.name: -json
options.description: Print a sequence of JSON objects to standard output, describing each downloaded module (or failure)

options.name: -x
options.description: Print the commands download executes to standard error

options.name: -module
options.description: Change the module's path

options.name: -go
options.description: Set the expected Go language version

options.name: -require
options.description: Add a requirement on the given module

options.name: -droprequire
options.description: Drop a requirement on the given module

options.name: -exclude
options.description: Add an exclusion on the given module

options.name: -dropexclude
options.description: Drop an exclusion on the given module

options.name: -replace
options.description: Add a replacement of the given module path and version pair

options.name: -dropreplace
options.description: Drops a replacement of the given module path and version pair

options.name: -retract
options.description: Add a retraction for the given version

options.name: -dropretract
options.description: Drop a retraction for the given version

options.name: -fmt
options.description: Format the go.mod file without making other changes

options.name: -print
options.description: Print the final go.mod in its text format instead of writing it back to disk

options.name: -json
options.description: Print the final go.mod in JSON format instead of writing it back to disk in text forma

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize and write a new go.mod file in the current directory

subcommands.name: tidy
subcommands.description: Ensure that the go.mod file matches the source code in the module

options.name: -e
options.description: Attempt to proceed despite errors encountered while loading packages

options.name: -v
options.description: Print information about removed modules

subcommands.name: vendor
subcommands.description: Construct a directory named vendor in the main module's root directory

options.name: -e
options.description: Attempt to proceed despite errors encountered while loading packages

options.name: -v
options.description: Print information about removed modules

subcommands.name: verify
subcommands.description: Check that dependencies of the main module stored in the module cache have not been modified since they were downloaded

subcommands.name: why
subcommands.description: Show a shortest path in the import graph from the main module to each of the listed packages

options.name: -m
options.description: Treat its arguments as a list of modules

options.name: -vendor
options.description: Ignore imports in tests of packages outside the main module

subcommands.name: edit
subcommands.description: Edit go.work from tools or scripts

options.name: -fmt
options.description: The -fmt flag reformats the go.work file without making other changes. This reformatting is also implied by any other modifications that use or rewrite the go.mod file. The only time this flag is needed is if no other flags are specified, as in 'go work edit -fmt'

options.name: -use
options.description: The -use=path and -dropuse=path flags add and drop a use directive from the go.work file's set of module directories

options.name: -dropuse
options.description: The -use=path and -dropuse=path flags add and drop a use directive from the go.work file's set of module directories

options.name: -replace
options.description: The -replace=old[@v]=new[@v] flag adds a replacement of the given module path and version pair. If the @v in old@v is omitted, a replacement without a version on the left side is added, which applies to all versions of the old module path. If the @v in new@v is omitted, the new path should be a local module root directory, not a module path. Note that -replace overrides any redundant replacements for old[@v], so omitting @v will drop existing replacements for specific versions

options.name: -dropreplace
options.description: The -dropreplace=old[@v] flag drops a replacement of the given module path and version pair. If the @v is omitted, a replacement without a version on the left side is dropped

options.name: -go
options.description: Set the expected Go language version

options.name: -print
options.description: The -print flag prints the final go.work in its text format instead of writing it back to go.mod

options.name: -json
options.description: The -json flag prints the final go.work file in JSON format instead of writing it back to go.mod

subcommands.name: sync
subcommands.description: Sync workspace build list to modules

options.name: -r
options.description: The -r flag searches recursively for modules in the argument directories, and the use command operates as if each of the directories were specified as arguments: namely, use directives will be added for directories that exist, and removed for directories that do not exist

options.name: -n
options.description: Print the commands but do not run them

options.name: -v
options.description: Print the names of packages as they are compiled

options.name: -x
options.description: Print the commands

options.description: A comma-separated list of build tags to consider satisfied during the build

options.description: Pass the remainder of the command line to the test binary

options.name: -c
options.description: Compile the test binary to pkg.test but do not run it

options.name: -exec
options.description: Invoke the binary using xprog

options.name: -i
options.description: Install packages that are dependencies of the test

options.name: -json
options.description: Convert test output to JSON suitable

options.name: -o
options.description: Compile the test binary to the named file

options.name: -n
options.description: Print the command that would be executed but not execute it

options.name: -m
options.description: Print each executable's embedded module version information

options.name: -v
options.description: Report unrecognized files

options.name: -n
options.description: Print the commands but do not run them

options.name: -v
options.description: Print the names of packages as they are compiled

options.name: -x
options.description: Print the commands

options.name: -tags
options.description: A comma-separated list of build tags to consider satisfied during the build

options.name: -toolexec
options.description: A program to use to invoke toolchain programs like vet and asm

options.name: -vettool
options.description: Select a different analysis tool with alternative or additional checks
